docs.title: PresentPy
docs.text:
Create slides from Jupyter Notebooks

PresentPy allows you to take your Jupyter Notebooks and turn them into PowerPoint slides that then can be integrated into your presentation decks.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
I strongly recommend using pipx to install PresentPy:

pipx install presentpy

Otherwise, you can install it using pip or your favorite package manager – the package is called presentpy.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
To turn a notebook into slides, simply run:

presentpy [OPTIONS] nb NOTEBOOK_PATH

It also works with Python scripts:

presentpy [OPTIONS] py SCRIPT_PATH

docs.title: Code configuration
docs.text:
You can configure the code cells to be displayed in the slides by adding a comment on the last line of the cell. The comment should start with #% and then you can add the following options:

  • title: The title of the slide
  • highlights: A comma separated list of lines to highlight, each highlight could be a number or a range of lines separated by a dash, e.g. 1,3-6,6-7

For example, take the following code:

def compute_hcf(x, y):
if x > y:
smaller = y
else:
smaller = x
for i in range(1, smaller+1):
if((x % i == 0) and (y % i == 0)):
hcf = i 
return hcf
#% title="Find the H.C.F of two numbers" highlights=1,2-3,4-5,9

The code above will generate 5 slides showing the same code but highlighting different lines in each slide – see how it looks below:

You can then use these slides in your presentation deck.

docs.title: Configuration file
docs.text: PresentPy can be configured externally using a configuration file. The configuration file is a TOML file that can be used to further customize the slides generated by PresentPy.

docs.title: Configuration file location
docs.text:
The configuration file can be placed in the following locations:

  • presentpy.toml in the current directory
  • presentpy.toml in the user's home directory
  • .presentpy.toml in the user's home directory

They are searched in the order above, so if you have a configuration file in the current directory, it will be used instead of the one in the user's home directory.
